My approach combines technical depth with a strong focus on collaboration and mentoring. I’m passionate about creating systems that empower people and simplify complexity.

person.name: Osman Bedrettin Yuce
person.bio: Osman Bedrettin Yüce is a Linux system administrator driven by a deep curiosity about how systems truly work behind the scenes. His path into cloud native technologies was shaped by years of hands on exploration. He started by building homelab infrastructures and experimenting with self hosted platforms, and later moved into working with production grade Kubernetes environments. For Osman, systems are not just tools; they are ecosystems to be explored, improved, and understood at a deeper level.

person.name: Shigeki Nara
person.bio: I have experience in deploying and operating container environments using Kubernetes and OpenShift, especially ROSA (Red Hat OpenShift on AWS). I also have experience in the design and implementation of AWS multi-account environments as an IT Architect and SRE.

person.name: Andrei Beșleagă
person.bio: I am a DevOps engineer with over four years of industry experience. Learning Kubernetes has been more than adopting a new framework—it represents a deep dive into modern cloud-native principles. Kubernetes brings together best practices in high availability, fault tolerance, and security, shaping the way I design and operate resilient systems.

person.name: Lori Karikari
person.bio: Lori is a Cloud Engineer in Belgium with a passion for Linux, Kubernetes and automation. Outside work, she's hopelessly addicted to her homelab Kubernetes cluster.

person.name: Mohamed Rabie
person.bio: I am a dedicated DevOps Engineer with a strong enthusiasm for mastering cutting-edge technologies, especially in the realm of Kubernetes and CNCF ecosystems. I actively participate in webinars and online seminars to stay abreast of the latest industry trends and best practices. While I do not currently lead any Meetups, I am constantly seeking opportunities to collaborate with like-minded professionals in the CNCF community.

person.name: Muhammad Mohsin Alvi
person.bio: Muhammad Mohsin Alvi is a DevOps and Platform Engineer with deep expertise in Kubernetes and CNCF technologies. He is a Kubestronaut, having earned all five Kubernetes certifications, with hands-on experience in Kubernetes security, service mesh (Istio), GitOps, and cloud-native networking. Mohsin works extensively with production Kubernetes environments, focusing on scalability, security hardening, and operational excellence. He is passionate about continuous learning, certification-driven mastery, and applying cloud-native best practices in real-world enterprise platforms.

person.name: Juliano Costa
person.bio: Juliano is a passionate Software Engineer/Developer Advocate with extensive experience in cloud-related technologies. He is specialized in OpenTelemetry and is always working with Kubernetes, Helm, Containers, Jenkins, and other related tools. As one of the maintainers of the CNCF project OpenTelemetry Demo, Juliano has helped to create a distributed system that demonstrates the implementation of OpenTelemetry in a near real-world environment. With a strong belief in the power of community, Juliano co-founded the Cloud Native Linz meetup. He actively participates in the tech community and is always willing to share his knowledge and insights with others. When not working, Juliano likes to spend his time with the family traveling around Austria and sightseeing new places.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I strongly believe in the power of the community and how it can help everyone grow, not just professionally, but as a whole. I'm extremely excited about being able to help growing the CNCF community and raising it awareness wherever I go. I love learning and sharing what I learn. The atmosphere and environment of openness created by CNCF is something that I take with me in every interaction I have. I'm super excited and thankful in becoming a CNCF Ambassador.

person.name: Keithen Adams
person.bio: As a platform engineer, I’m passionate about continuous learning and always seeking opportunities to deepen my technical knowledge and broaden my skill set.

person.name: Robin Werner

My focus is on developing modern cloud strategies, with a focus in Kubernetes. In the past, I have led numerous cloud projects, built strategic partnerships, and conducted workshops and training sessions. Innovation, customer orientation, and continuous learning are at the core of my approach.

person.name: Shih Jia Lai
person.bio: A cloud-native architect dedicated to helping traditional enterprises transition from virtualization to containerization. Passionate about modernizing infrastructure and embracing DevOps and cloud-native practices. Actively promoting CNCF technologies and culture, with the goal of enabling more organizations to build fast, secure, and scalable architectures that accelerate innovation.

person.name: Tsend-Ayush Sharavdorj
person.bio: With over 10 years of experience in software development and architecture, I currently work as a Solution Architect at Novelsoft in Ulaanbaatar. My core expertise spans cloud-native systems, API management, database optimization, data warehouse and lakehouse solutions, and enterprise integration.

person.name: Marcus Noble
person.bio: Marcus is a platform engineer and his main area of focus in recent years has been around CI/CD, Go, Kubernetes, containers and DevOps but originally started out as a web developer and JavaScript enthusiast. A self-described “tinkerer”, when not building Kubernetes solutions, Marcus likes to dabble with 3D printing and experimenting with smart home tech. He can be found blogging online at www.MarcusNoble.co.uk and giving talks at meetups & conferences.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I am excited to leverage the support of the CNCF to better help others within out community through shared knowledge and experience. I'm excited for the opportunity to meet more amazing people in the cloud native space and learn from their experiences and expertise.

person.name: Adam Rogers
person.bio: Just a dude who likes tech things and burgers

person.name: Baba Prasad Pendyala
person.bio: I am an Enterprise Cloud Architect and Platform Engineering leader at Walmart Global Tech, with deep experience in Kubernetes, cloud-native technologies, and large-scale multi-cloud platforms. My work focuses on designing secure, scalable developer platforms and driving modernization initiatives across AWS, Azure, and GCP. I regularly share insights through technical writing on topics such as GitHub Advanced Security, DevSecOps automation, Kubernetes operations, and cloud governance. I’m passionate about building reliable, high-performing cloud platforms that empower engineering teams and enable enterprise transformation.

person.name: Christian Huth

My focus is on Automation, GitOps, Kubernetes and Monitoring.  Sharing my knowledge is my Passion. Therefore I hold Trainings on Kubernetes and write articles on my Blog about Cloud Native Technologies.

Additionally, I advocate for kindness, support for people transitioning to a career in tech -- especially women, DEI, BIPOC, and work-life balance for all parents in this space.
